Mouse Forecast

Current season

House mouse abundance across Australian grain-growing regions

Overview

In short

  • Geraldton and Kwinana regions (WA): PREPARE TO ACT AT SOWING
  • Adelaide Plains (SA): WATCH AND ACT AT SOWING
  • Northern NSW and southern QLD: KEEP WATCH

In detail

  • Western Australia: widespread and concerning reports of very high numbers, primarily around Geraldton. Recent monitoring suggests this extends to the Kwinana West region. We are currently uncertain about other regions in the state.

  • Adelaide Plains: recent survey data provides strong evidence of widespread, moderate-high (and increasing) population densities. There is some potential for an outbreak depending on future conditions.

  • Low-moderate numbers seen across northern NSW and southern QLD from recent rapid assessment monitoring, although some farmer reports have come in recently.

  • Low mouse numbers in Victoria, southern & central west NSW.

Map: survey data

  • AE zone
  • Region

  • Mouse activity is high in Adelaide Plains, Kwinana West and Yorke Peninsula.

  • Mouse activity is moderate in Albany, Coonamble, Downs Northern, Esperance and Goondiwindi.

  • All 20 recently-surveyed regions have current-season data.

Management reccomendations

  1. Actively gauge mouse numbers by walking through paddocks (mouse chew cards and active burrow counts are useful tools at this time of year).
  2. At 6 weeks prior to sowing (now): If mouse activity is high and there is plenty of background food, reduce the background food through light tillage or sheep grazing, and strategically bait with ZnP25 as the only currently registered ZnP bait available. If mice are still a problem at sowing, be prepared to bait again at that time, ensuring a 6-week gap to overcome any behavioural aversion from ingesting a sub-lethal dose.
  3. At sowing: If mice remain a problem at sowing, apply bait off the back of the seeder to prevent damage to the freshly sown crop.
  4. Baiting at sowing is most effective if no other food sources are available.
  5. Talk to bait suppliers early to ensure timely supply. As with any agricultural chemical, use the product in accordance with the label instructions, and report any adverse or off target effects via the APVMA website.

About

Mouse forecasts are possible through GRDC’s investment in CSP2501-006RTX (Mouse surveillance and monitoring to inform leading practice).

Monitoring data is collected 2 - 3 times a year at each long-term monitoring site. Using live-traps, active burrow seaches and chew card surveys.

Data is collected by CSIRO, NSW Local Land Services, NSW Department of Primary Industries and Rural Development, Central West Farming Services, Birchip Cropping Group, & Farmanco. We particularly thank NSW DPIRD who generously provide additional live-trapping survey data. Further live-trapping data is also supplied through the CSP2501-006RTX (Mouse Ecology project).

Forecasts will be updated seasonally and as new data comes available. We are actively developing new statistical models to improve mouse forecasts, and working on their presentation.

Mouse forecasts are put together through consultation with the National Mouse Group (NMG), a grower-led group made up of 20-30 members who meet 3x a year to discuss the current mouse situation and develop management recommendations. The NMG comprises grower representatives, state government staff, grain industry representatives, GRDC and CSIRO.
